title: Problem-Driven Design Patterns · Runtime Lens: Oop Command Pattern
summary: Add pricing, notifications, imports, commands, and persistence without turning patterns into ceremony. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oop command pattern increment in an extensible library operations toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent operations as command values when queuing, logging, undo, authorization, or scheduling justifies it.
Publish domain events to decouple secondary reactions while defining ordering, failure, duplication, and delivery semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
